commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=924acdeffaa59660ea66b75951e77eae19e98f23)
commands.review_summary:
Adds positional cron add/create schedule and prompt parsing, exposes `--webhook` on cron add/create/edit, clears delivery fields across webhook/chat mode changes, updates cron docs, and widens schedule identity input types.

PR surface: Source +118, Tests +196, Docs +30, Other +12. Total +356 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows `cron add/create --webhook --system-event` and `cron edit --webhook` can store webhook delivery for main jobs, while `executeMainSessionCronJob` only queues a system event and never calls webhook dispatch.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose the main-session webhook contract and require live webhook proof before merge; this is not a cleanup close or a safe automated merge as-is.

target.title: feat: improve cron create delivery ergonomics
target.body:
## Summary

- Add Hermes-style `openclaw cron create "0 2 * * *" "prompt" --name ...` parsing for cron expressions, `every <duration>` intervals, durations, and timestamps while preserving the existing flagged create path.
- Keep `--webhook <url>` delivery on `cron add/create` and `cron edit`, with conflicts against chat delivery flags.
- Clear stale delivery fields when cron jobs cross the webhook/chat boundary, including webhook-to-announce edits and `--no-deliver` edits on main webhook jobs.
- Update cron CLI and scheduled-task docs with positional schedule/prompt examples and webhook examples.
- Widen cron schedule identity comparison types to accept persisted numeric schedule inputs that the normalizer already supports, while preserving cron stagger semantics.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/cron/schedule-identity.test.ts src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm test src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm check:import-cycles`
- `pnpm check:changed` via Crabbox `run_5057742101ad` / `harbor-barnacle` / exit 0
- `pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check`
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-skills/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (final run clean)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: cron creation can use the Hermes-style schedule-first and prompt-second command shape, plus webhook delivery without hand-editing job JSON or relying on undocumented delivery fields.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout plus delegated Crabbox changed gate on Linux.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/cron/schedule-identity.test.ts src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `pnpm test src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts src/cron/service.jobs.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `pnpm check:test-types`; `pnpm check:import-cycles`; `pnpm check:changed`; `pnpm check:docs`; `git diff --check`; autoreview branch diff.

Evidence after fix: cron CLI regression tests assert positional cron schedules, positional `every 1h` schedules, positional prompt conflicts, positional names, webhook add/create payloads, webhook edit payloads, `--no-deliver` as a delivery-only edit, and mixed webhook/chat delivery errors. Cron service regression tests assert switching to webhook delivery clears chat-only fields and switching back to announce clears stale webhook targets unless an explicit chat target is supplied. Test type checking covers the persisted schedule identity type shape used by current CI merge refs.

Observed result after fix: all targeted tests and changed gates passed; final autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

What was not tested: live Gateway cron execution against an external webhook endpoint.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
